blog_articles.title: Creamy Nettles Soup in 30 Minutes
blog_articles.excerpt: A simple, warming nettles soup you can make in half an hour from freshly foraged greens.
blog_articles.content:
This creamy nettles soup is one of the easiest ways to turn a basket of fresh spring nettles into a nourishing meal. In this recipe we'll cover how to safely handle nettles, how to blanch them to remove the sting, and how to build a rich flavour base with onions, garlic and potatoes.

Safety note: always wear gloves when harvesting and handling raw nettles. Once blanched, the sting disappears.

Serve this soup with crusty bread, a drizzle of olive oil, and a squeeze of lemon.
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook for 5–7 minutes until translucent.
Stir in the garlic and potatoes and cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable stock, bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer for 10 minutes.
While the potatoes simmer, blanch the nettles in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain and squeeze out excess water.
Roughly chop the nettles and add them to the pan. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Blend the soup until smooth, then stir in the cream or coconut milk.
Season with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon to taste. Serve hot.

blog_articles.title: Quick Nettles Pesto for Pasta or Toast
blog_articles.excerpt: Turn a handful of young nettles into a vibrant green pesto in under 20 minutes.
blog_articles.content:
Nettles make an earthy, mineral-rich alternative to basil in classic pesto. This recipe shows you how to safely prepare nettles and blitz them into a versatile sauce for pasta, grains, or toast.

Once blanched, nettles lose their sting but retain their deep green colour and flavour. Toasted nuts and good olive oil round everything out.
ingredients: 1/3 cup toasted sunflower seeds or pine nuts
2 tbsp nutritional yeast or grated Parmesan
steps: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Wearing gloves, add nettles and blanch for 60–90 seconds.
Drain nettles and rinse under cold water, then squeeze out excess moisture.
Add nettles, toasted seeds, garlic, lemon juice, and nutritional yeast or Parmesan to a food processor.
Pulse while drizzling in olive oil until a thick, spoonable paste forms.
Season with salt and adjust lemon to taste.
Serve immediately or store in the fridge in a jar topped with a thin layer of oil.

blog_articles.title: Oven-Baked Nettles and Wild Garlic Frittata
blog_articles.excerpt: A protein-rich brunch frittata featuring spring nettles and wild garlic, baked in the oven.
blog_articles.content:
This oven-baked frittata packs early spring greens into a simple one-pan meal. Nettles, wild garlic, and new potatoes combine for a hearty breakfast, brunch, or light dinner.

Use any mild wild greens you have on hand if you cannot find wild garlic. Always double-check your identifications before eating anything from the wild.
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C / 355°F.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe frying pan and sauté the onion until soft.
Add the potatoes and cook for 3–4 minutes until lightly golden.
Stir in the chopped nettles and wild garlic, cooking for another 2 minutes.
Whisk the eggs in a bowl with salt, pepper, and grated cheese.
Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables in the pan and cook on the hob for 2–3 minutes until the edges start to set.
Drizzle with the remaining olive oil and transfer the pan to the oven.
Bake for 15–18 minutes until just set in the centre. Rest for 5 minutes before slicing.

blog_articles.title: Mushroom Foraging Safety: 7 Rules to Follow
blog_articles.excerpt: Before you join a mushroom walk, learn the essential safety rules every forager should know.
blog_articles.content:
Mushroom foraging can be deeply rewarding, but it carries real risks if you are not careful. In this article we cover seven core rules: never eat anything you cannot confidently identify, avoid white-gilled mushrooms, understand lookalikes, start with a small tasting, keep specimens separate, use multiple sources to verify IDs, and when in doubt, throw it out.

We also explain why guided mushroom walks are the safest way to begin, letting you learn from experienced instructors in the field.

blog_articles.title: Beginner’s Guide to Foraging Common Wild Herbs
blog_articles.excerpt: Learn how to safely find, identify, and use 10 easy wild herbs, from cleavers to yarrow.
blog_articles.content:
If you are new to herbal foraging, start with abundant, easily recognisable plants. In this guide we cover cleavers, yarrow, plantain, chickweed, dandelion, and more. You will learn basic ID features, habitat, ethical harvesting, and simple ways to use each plant.

We also explain how to keep a foraging journal and why taking clear photographs is essential when you are starting out.

blog_articles.title: Family-Friendly Foraging: Tips for Walks with Kids
blog_articles.excerpt: Make your next foraging walk with children safe, fun, and stress‑free with these simple tips.
blog_articles.content:
Foraging with children can spark a lifelong connection with nature. To keep things enjoyable, choose easy terrain, short routes, and species that are simple to recognise. Bring snacks, water, spare layers, and a small basket for each child.

In this article we share age‑appropriate activities, safety rules to explain to kids, and ideas for what to do with your finds when you get home.

blog_articles.title: Coastal Foraging Basics: Tides, Zones, and Safety
blog_articles.excerpt: Heading on a coastal foraging retreat? Learn how to read tides, identify safe zones, and avoid common hazards.
blog_articles.content:
Coastal foraging opens up a new world of edible seaweeds, shellfish, and shoreline plants. Before you head to the shore, it’s crucial to understand tide times, legal access, and pollution risks.

This guide explains tidal ranges, spring vs neap tides, and how to stay aware of incoming water. We also cover safe shellfish guidelines and why local knowledge is invaluable.

blog_articles.title: What to Forage Around Seattle in July
blog_articles.excerpt: Berries, seaweeds, and fresh greens: a month‑by‑month foraging guide for Seattle in July.
blog_articles.content:
July in the Seattle area is peak season for berries like thimbleberries, salmonberries, and trailing blackberries. Higher elevations may still hold young greens, while low tides reveal sea lettuces and kelps along Puget Sound.

This article outlines key species, lookalikes, and ethical harvesting tips specific to the Seattle region in midsummer.

blog_articles.title: 5 Common Plant Identification Mistakes (and How to Avoid Them)
blog_articles.excerpt: New to plant ID? Learn the most frequent mistakes beginners make in the field and how to correct them.
blog_articles.content:
From relying on flower colour alone to ignoring habitat, beginners often overlook key features when identifying plants. This article walks through five common mistakes and gives you practical ways to slow down, observe more, and build reliable IDs.

We also suggest simple exercises you can do on walks to strengthen your observation skills.

blog_articles.title: Skillet Wild Berry Crumble (Campfire-Friendly)
blog_articles.excerpt: Use blackberries, huckleberries, or other wild fruits to make a simple crumble in 45 minutes.
blog_articles.content: This crumble can be baked in a home oven or cooked over hot coals in a cast-iron skillet. It’s flexible enough for whatever berries you find in season. We cover safe berry choices, prepping your pan, and cooking options for both home and campfire.
steps: Toss berries with sugar and lemon juice and spread in a greased skillet.
Mix oats, flour, brown sugar, and cinnamon in a bowl.
Rub in butter or coconut oil until crumbs form.
Sprinkle topping over berries.
Bake at 180°C / 355°F for 30–35 minutes, or cover and cook over campfire coals until bubbling and golden.

blog_articles.title: How to Use a Field Guide on Foraging Walks
blog_articles.excerpt: Make the most of your field guide in the field with these simple, practical strategies.
blog_articles.content:
A good field guide is more than just a book of pictures. In this article we show you how to use keys, distribution maps, and habitat notes to narrow down your identifications.

We also explain why it’s helpful to mark up your guide with notes, tabs, and dates of your finds, and how to pair a physical guide with digital resources.

blog_articles.title: 7 Wild Food Gift Ideas for Nature-Loving Friends
blog_articles.excerpt: From guided walks to field guides, here are seven thoughtful gifts for the forager in your life.
blog_articles.content:
If you’re shopping for someone who loves wild food, experiences often make the best gifts. We share ideas for guided mushroom walks, online course vouchers, high‑quality field guides, and small items like waxed foraging bags.

Most of these gifts can be delivered digitally or by post, making them ideal last‑minute options.

courses.title: Beginner Herbal Foraging: From Lawn to Teacup
courses.subtitle: Learn to safely identify and use 12 common wild herbs around your home.
courses.description: This self-paced online course introduces you to the fundamentals of herbal foraging. Through video lessons, printable worksheets, and quizzes, you’ll learn to find, identify, harvest, and use 12 beginner-friendly herbs for teas, infusions, and simple remedies.

courses.title: Wild Herbs 101: Backyard and Park Plants
courses.subtitle: A gentle introduction to urban herbal foraging for complete beginners.
courses.description: Designed for city-dwellers, this self-paced course focuses on herbs you can find in parks, verges, and back gardens. Learn ID basics, safety, legal considerations, and how to turn your finds into teas, vinegars, and simple salves.

courses.title: Four-Season Herbal Foraging for Beginners
courses.subtitle: Build confidence identifying medicinal plants all year round.
courses.description: This comprehensive beginner course is organised by season, helping you discover new herbs from spring to winter. Includes 40+ video lessons, field assignments, and downloadable ID sheets.

courses.title: Intermediate Mushroom Identification
courses.subtitle: Take your mushroom ID skills beyond the basics.
courses.description: For foragers who know the common edible mushrooms and want to go deeper, this course explores more challenging groups, dangerous lookalikes, and advanced identification techniques.

courses.title: Plant Identification for Foragers (Beginner)
courses.subtitle: Learn the fundamentals of plant ID using real-world field examples.

courses.title: Wild Cookery: Cooking with Foraged Ingredients
courses.subtitle: Turn your foraged finds into delicious meals.
courses.description: An all-levels course that focuses on cooking techniques, preservation methods, and flexible recipes for common wild foods.

courses.title: Manchester Autumn Mushrooms Weekend Intensive
courses.subtitle: Two live online sessions plus one in-person field day near Manchester.
courses.description: A hybrid course combining two live online theory evenings with a guided mushroom foraging day in woodlands near Manchester.

courses.title: Seattle Intro to Urban Foraging (In-Person)
courses.subtitle: A weekend course exploring edible plants in Seattle parks.
courses.description: Join us in person for two half-days of guided plant walks, focusing on beginner-friendly species in and around Seattle.

courses.title: Advanced Herbal Field Botany
courses.subtitle: For experienced foragers ready to dive into botanical keys.
courses.description: This advanced course teaches you how to use technical floras and keys to identify challenging plants to species level.

courses.title: Free Mini-Series: Three Wild Plants to Know
courses.subtitle: A short taster course introducing three easy, safe plants.
courses.description: This free mini-course offers three video lessons introducing dandelion, plantain, and nettles. It’s an ideal taster before committing to a longer course.

experiences.title: Manchester Spring Mushroom Foraging Walk

experiences.whatsIncludedSummary: 3‑hour guided woodland mushroom walk, safety briefing, and identification handout.

experiences.title: Peak District Mushroom Ramble from Manchester

experiences.title: Beginner Plant Identification Walk in the Downs

experiences.description: Learn the basics of plant identification on this short guided walk. We will focus on 8–10 easy species, teaching you how to observe leaves, stems, and habitat.
experiences.whatsIncludedSummary: 2.5‑hour guided walk, printed ID checklist, and follow‑up email with notes.

experiences.title: Advanced Plant Identification Day Workshop

experiences.description: For confident foragers ready to use botanical keys, this 7‑hour workshop spends most of the day in the field learning in-depth plant ID techniques.

experiences.title: Seattle Family Foraging Morning in the Park
experiences.subtitle: Kid‑friendly forage focusing on berries and simple greens.

experiences.title: Forest Edge Berries & Birds Family Walk
experiences.subtitle: Morning stroll combining berry foraging and simple bird spotting.
experiences.description: Ideal for families who love wildlife, this walk blends berry foraging with easy bird identification along a forest edge trail near Seattle.
experiences.whatsIncludedSummary: 2‑hour guided walk with berry tasting and simple bird ID activities.

experiences.title: 3‑Day Coastal Foraging & Cooking Retreat (Cornwall)

experiences.title: Oregon Coast 3‑Day Wild Seafoods Retreat

experiences.whatsIncludedSummary: 3 days of guided coastal foraging, 2 nights accommodation, 2 breakfasts, 1 lunch, and 2 dinners.

experiences.title: Private Group Mushroom Foraging Walk (Custom)
experiences.subtitle: Design a private mushroom walk for your group with a dedicated guide.
experiences.description: Perfect for birthdays, team days, or special occasions. Choose your preferred date, location region, and difficulty, and our team will match you with a guide for a custom mushroom foraging experience.
experiences.whatsIncludedSummary: 3‑hour private guided mushroom walk tailored to your group.

experiences.title: Manchester Canal-Side Urban Foraging Walk
experiences.subtitle: Discover edible plants along Manchester’s waterways.
experiences.description: A relaxed urban foraging walk exploring edible weeds, trees, and shrubs along canal paths close to Manchester city centre.
experiences.whatsIncludedSummary: 2‑hour guided urban foraging walk with ID handouts.

experiences.title: Portland Forest Park Edible Plants Walk
experiences.subtitle: Intermediate hike discovering Pacific Northwest edibles.
experiences.description: A half‑day guided walk through Forest Park near Portland, OR, focusing on regional edible and medicinal plants.
experiences.whatsIncludedSummary: 4‑hour guided woodland walk with regional plant focus.
